content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me examine atom index 2 (C:2) carefully.

From the image, I can clearly see that atom C:2 is bonded to:
- C:1 (carbon, index 1) - to the left
- C:3 (carbon, index 3) - to the right  
- H:30 (hydrogen, index 30) - above C:2

The connections are clearly visible as covalent bonds (the orange sticks connecting the atoms). C:2 has three neighbors total, which makes sense for a carbon atom in this molecular structure.

Based on the file name "geomconf_3792.xyz", the geomconf_index is 3792.

So the neighbor atoms of atom index 2 (C:2) are:
- Atom index 1, element C
- Atom index 3, element C  
- Atom index 30, element H

geomconf_index: 3792

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 2, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
